title: Let the first equation be (1), the second equation be (2), and the third equation be (3). Subtract (2) from (1) to get (4). Subtract (3) from 3(2) to get (5).
From (5), solve for $$y$$ and plug into (4) to get $$x$$. Plug $$x$$ back into $$y$$ from (5) to get $$y$$. Plug $$x$$ and $$y$$ into (2) to get $$z$$.
